title: Brute-Force a Weak, Unthrottled Login
objective: Brute-force the login for a low-security account with no rate limiting or lockout.
briefing: The /login endpoint accepts unlimited attempts with no delay or lockout. The '{victim_username}' account probably uses a common, weak password.
hints: With no lockout or rate limit, you can just keep guessing passwords freely.
Try a very common weak password rather than something clever.
debrief: The login endpoint never limited failed attempts, so guessing common passwords one after another was never slowed down or blocked - eventually a common weak password ('letmein') matched. This is an Identification and Authentication Failure (OWASP A07:2021): authentication existed, but nothing defended it against brute-forcing or credential stuffing. The fix is layered - account lockout or exponential backoff after repeated failures, rate limiting per IP/account, and ideally MFA so a correct password guess alone isn't enough to log in.

logs_append: [AUTH] {victim_username} account compromised via brute force - no lockout in place
[AUTH] failed login for '{victim_username}' - no lockout triggered
